howTo.name: How to use the RGB to HSL converter

steps.text: Type R, G, B values (0–255 each).

steps.name: Copy values
steps.text: Copy HSL values for CSS or design use.

contentBlocks.title: When do you need to convert RGB to HSL?
contentBlocks.html:
HSL (Hue, Saturation, Lightness) is an alternative way to specify colors that is more intuitive for humans. Hue is the color angle (0–360°), Saturation is the color intensity (0–100%), and Lightness is how light/dark (0–100%).

HSL makes it easy to create color variations: adjust lightness for tints/shades, adjust saturation for muted/vivid versions, or shift hue for complementary colors.

CSS supports hsl() natively: hsl(14, 100%, 60%) is equivalent to rgb(255, 87, 51) and #FF5733.

All calculations run locally in your browser — nothing is sent to any server.

items.title: 1. Enter a value
items.description: Type a number in the input field. You can use a period or comma as the decimal separator.

items.title: 2. Read the result
items.description: The conversion result appears instantly in the field next to it – no clicking required.

items.title: 3. Copy or reverse
items.description: Click Copy result or use the Reverse button to convert in the opposite direction.

items.title: Palety kolorów
items.description: Tworzenie wariantów: zmieniaj L (jasność) przy stałym H i S.

items.title: Tryb ciemny
items.description: Zmiana L z 95% na 10% tworzy wariant ciemnego motywu.

items.title: Projektowanie
items.description: HSL ułatwia tworzenie spójnych palet: obróć H o 120° = kolor komplementarny.

items.title: Complete privacy
items.description: All calculations run locally in your browser. No data is sent to any server.

items.title: Real-time results
items.description: The result updates instantly as you type – no need to click any button.

contentBlocks.title: How does RGB to HSL conversion work?
contentBlocks.html:
Normalize R, G, B to 0–1. Find max and min channel values. L = (max + min) / 2. S depends on L: if L ≤ 0.5, S = (max−min)/(max+min); if L > 0.5, S = (max−min)/(2−max−min).

Hue depends on which channel is max: if R is max, H = (G−B)/(max−min); if G is max, H = 2 + (B−R)/(max−min); if B is max, H = 4 + (R−G)/(max−min). Multiply by 60°.

HSL is fully reversible — converting HSL back to RGB produces the exact same color.

contentBlocks.title: Practical tips
contentBlocks.html:
• Red: hsl(0, 100%, 50%). Green: hsl(120, 100%, 50%). Blue: hsl(240, 100%, 50%).
  • Complementary color: add 180° to hue. Red (0°) → Cyan (180°).
  • Lighten: increase L%. Darken: decrease L%. L=0% = black, L=100% = white.
  • Desaturate: decrease S%. S=0% = grayscale. S=100% = pure color.

items.question: What is HSL?
items.answer: Hue (color angle 0–360°), Saturation (intensity 0–100%), Lightness (brightness 0–100%). A more intuitive way to work with colors.

items.question: How do I darken a color in HSL?
items.answer: Reduce the L value. hsl(200, 80%, 50%) → hsl(200, 80%, 30%) is a darker version of the same blue.

items.question: What is the complementary color?
items.answer: Add 180° to the hue. hsl(200, 80%, 50%) → hsl(20, 80%, 50%) is the complementary color.

items.question: Is HSL the same as HSV/HSB?
items.answer: No. HSL and HSV/HSB use different formulas. L=50% is pure color, while V/B=100% is pure color. They produce different results.

items.question: Are calculations done locally?
items.answer: Yes. All calculations run in your browser. No data is sent to any server.
